channel.title: Raj Macwan — Blog
channel.description: Notes on infrastructure, cybersecurity, AI, and leadership from a practitioner's desk.
channel.copyright: © 2026 Raj Macwan. Views are my own.

item.title: Why I Built a Dark Web Research Site (and What I Learned)
item.description: Running a Tor hidden service as a senior IT leader. Stack notes, tradecraft, identity separation, and what the project actually taught me about privacy engineering.

item.title: The CISA KEV Catalog as a Prioritized Patch Backlog
item.description: Most patch management programs drown in CVE noise. CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list is the strongest prioritization signal available, and how to actually integrate it into workflow.

item.title: AI in IT Operations: A Reality Check from the Trenches
item.description: A balanced practitioner view on where AI actually works in IT operations, where it is hype, and the governance problems nobody wants to own.

item.title: MIT Week 1: Back in a Classroom After 20 Years
item.description: Returning to formal study at MIT after two decades in industry. Early notes on the gap between running the work and studying it.

item.title: Zero Trust Is Harder Than Vendors Admit
item.description: The Zero Trust architecture is sound. The products sold under its name often are not. A pragmatic five-year view of what the real journey looks like.
